talk_title: Mechanisms of ADC resistance informing novel combination therapy strategies

models.name: TNBC cell lines with WT or mutant TROP2

MOA_summary: SG (sacituzumab govitecan) binds TROP2, is internalized, releases SN-38 topoisomerase I inhibitor; resistance arises via TROP2 missense mutations that mislocalize the antigen and TOP1 point mutations that prevent SN-38 binding. Sequential PARP inhibition after SG exposure stabilizes TOP1 cleavage complexes, enhancing cytotoxicity.
key_findings: Acquired TROP2 missense mutations redirect the protein intracellularly, conferring resistance to SG.
TOP1 E418K mutation, previously unseen in patients, mediates resistance to the SN-38 payload.
TOP1 mutations detected in cfDNA precede clinical progression, serving as a potential biomarker.
Sequential dosing of SG followed by talazoparib is tolerable and yields durable responses, whereas concurrent dosing causes high myelosuppression.
PARP inhibition after SG washout synergistically stabilizes TOP1 cleavage complexes, enhancing tumor cell killing.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: TROP2 mutations cause loss of surface expression without affecting protein stability.
TOP1 mutations cluster at the DNA‑protein interface, reducing SN-38 intercalation.

combination_strategies.rationale: PARP inhibition stabilizes TOP1 cleavage complexes after SG-induced DNA damage
combination_strategies.phenotype: Synergistic cytotoxicity in vitro; improved durability in sequential clinical dosing

open_questions: Will selective PARP1 inhibitors allow concurrent SG + PARP inhibition with acceptable toxicity?
Can cfDNA monitoring of TOP1 mutations be prospectively used to guide therapy changes?

talk_title: Tumor intrinsic and extrinsic mechanisms of ADC resistance in bladder cancer

biomarkers.name: NECTIN4 H-score
biomarkers.finding: Higher H-score correlates with longer PFS and OS on EV

models.name: Bladder cancer lines with variable NECTIN4
EV-resistant PDX models
models.genotype_or_key_features: Upregulated NECTIN4 but altered lysosomal trafficking

MOA_summary: Enfortumab vedotin targets NECTIN4, is internalized and releases MMAE; resistance mechanisms include low or heterogeneous NECTIN4 expression, payload efflux, and altered endosomal trafficking. NECTIN4 expression can be pharmacologically up‑regulated via PPARγ agonists (rosiglitazone) or retinoic‑acid signaling (ATRA/Bexarotene), enhancing ADC efficacy.
key_findings: NECTIN4 high tumors (H‑score >225) achieve median OS of 31.5 months versus 18.4 months in low expressors.
Chromosome 1 amplification of PVRL4 (NECTIN4) drives homogeneous membranous expression and superior clinical outcomes.
Rosiglitazone induces NECTIN4 surface expression in low‑expressing bladder cancer cells without affecting normal urothelium, sensitizing them to EV.
Retinoic‑acid agonists (ATRA, Bexarotene) similarly up‑regulate NECTIN4 via STRA6‑RXR signaling.
EV‑resistant models retain or increase NECTIN4 but show reduced ADC internalization due to altered lysosomal trafficking.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: EV‑resistant cells may up‑regulate drug efflux transporters (MDR1) and display impaired endosomal flux.
NECITN4 up‑regulation does not increase toxicity in normal bladder epithelial cells or keratinocytes.

combination_strategies.rationale: Priming tumor cells to increase NECTIN4 surface density before EV administration
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced tumor growth inhibition in vitro and in vivo

combination_strategies.rationale: Activate STRA6‑RXR pathway to boost NECTIN4 expression
combination_strategies.phenotype: Left‑shifted EV IC50 in cell lines

open_questions: What is the optimal clinical dosing schedule for PPARγ or retinoid priming with EV?
Can non‑invasive biomarkers (soluble NECTIN4, ctDNA) reliably track target up‑regulation?

talk_title: HER2 ADC resistance mechanisms and combination strategies

targets.alteration: point mutations in trastuzumab binding site

biomarkers.name: HER2 IHC score
biomarkers.finding: Higher HER2 expression correlates with greater T‑DXd internalization and response

models.name: HER2 WT and mutant overexpression lines

MOA_summary: Trastuzumab deruxtecan binds HER2, is internalized, and releases a topoisomerase‑I inhibitor; resistance occurs via HER2 point mutations that impair antibody binding, reduced HER2 surface density, and heterodimerization with EGFR that limits internalization. EGFR blockade or activation can modulate HER2 internalization and restore sensitivity.
key_findings: HER2 binding‑site mutations prevent T‑DXd internalization and confer resistance in vitro.
Doxycycline‑induced HER2 down‑regulation reduces ADC internalization and increases IC50 by ~800‑fold.
EGFR overexpression decreases HER2 homodimers, reducing T‑DXd efficacy; EGFR knockdown or cetuximab treatment restores HER2 internalization.
Combining cetuximab with T‑DXd improves tumor responses in HER2‑high/EGFR‑high models in vivo.
CAR‑T targeting HER2 remains effective against EV‑resistant cells that retain HER2 expression.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: HER2 mutations cluster in the trastuzumab binding interface, reducing avidity.
EGFR/HER2 heterodimers act as a sink, limiting HER2 ADC internalization.

combination_strategies.rationale: Promotes HER2 internalization by disrupting EGFR/HER2 heterodimers
Dual targeting delivers more payload via independent internalization routes
combination_strategies.phenotype: Comparable efficacy at lower doses of each ADC in vitro and in vivo

open_questions: What is the optimal sequencing of HER2‑ADC and EGFR‑targeted therapy in patients?
Can spatial profiling identify micro‑regional HER2 loss that predicts resistance?
